note: Scores are directional estimates based on common production-readiness practices. A passing score does not replace legal, security, or compliance review before launch.

scenarios.name: MVP prototype
scenarios.description: Low-risk internal demo with no customer data.

scenarios.name: Internal tool
scenarios.description: Used by employees, limited blast radius.

scenarios.name: Customer-facing agent
scenarios.description: Touches end users, revenue, or public data.

scenarios.name: Regulated industry
scenarios.description: Finance, healthcare, HR, or public-sector deployments.

items.text: Clear agent goal and success metric defined
Agent loop (plan → act → observe) is explicit and bounded
State/session management is persistent and recoverable
Tool definitions, permissions, and error contracts are documented
Input/output schemas validated before and after the agent runs
Human-in-the-loop trigger and escalation path defined
Primary model chosen for capability, cost, and latency fit
Latency and cost benchmarks measured for the target workload
Context window budgeted (system + RAG + history + output reserve)
Fallback model or provider configured for outages/rate limits
Local/quantized route evaluated if data cannot leave the environment
Data classification (public, internal, confidential, restricted) mapped
PII handling policy: masking, filtering, or blocking in prompts
Health/financial/biometric data reviewed for HIPAA, GLBA, or sector rules
Authentication, authorization, and least-privilege access controls in place
Data retention, model-call logging, and deletion policy defined
User consent and disclosure notices reviewed
DPAs and subprocessor list reviewed for cloud providers
System prompt and tool interface reviewed for injection / hijack risks
Adversarial test set or red-team run completed with accepted residual risk
Output integrity checks (refusal, grounding, citation) implemented
Dependencies, model weights, and packages pinned and scanned
Error handling and graceful degradation path defined
Provider rate limits and backoff strategy configured
Circuit breaker or kill-switch exists for runaway loops
Idempotency keys for mutating tool calls
Deployment region and failover plan aligned with data-residency needs
Structured logs captured for every agent run
Distributed traces or step-level spans available
Key metrics tracked: latency, cost, errors, escalations
Alerts for error rate, cost, and latency thresholds
Audit log retained for compliance and incident review
Per-request and monthly API budget set
Human fallback and incident cost estimated
Cost review cadence and ownership assigned
Launch checklist covers feature, security, and ops sign-offs
Rollback and feature-flag plan documented
On-call runbook with common failure modes
SLA/SLO targets communicated to stakeholders
Legal/security/compliance sign-off obtained if required

levels.label: Production ready
levels.summary: Launch with standard monitoring. Schedule a 30-day post-launch review.

levels.label: Launch candidate
levels.summary: Can launch with a feature flag or limited rollout. Close the open P0s before full traffic.

levels.label: Needs hardening
levels.summary: Fix missing P0 items and add observability before any customer-facing launch.

levels.label: High risk
levels.summary: Do not launch. Address architecture, security, and reliability gaps first.

levels.label: Not ready
levels.summary: Revisit the fundamentals: goal, model, loop, and data handling.
